channel.title: Workshop on Daoyin Chuan
channel.copyright: This work is licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0 International License.

item.title: 2012 Workshop Update
item.description: I talked with Sifu Fong Ha last night and we are definitely having the workshop this year in mid-August.  As I get the details firmed up I will post the at this web-site.

item.title: 2012 Workshop with Sifu Fong Ha
item.description:
We will be holding the Daoyin Chuan 2012 Workshop with Sifu Fong Ha on August 17th and 18th.  Friday (17th) night will be from 7:30pm to 10pm and Saturday (18th) will be from 9:30am to 5pm.  We will go to a local restaurant for our lunch break so be sure to bring money or a brown bag.  Either way we will be eating lunch together.  The location for the workshop is at The Grateful Pose (Yoga Center) at 845 N 400 E, Bountiful, UT.

The cost will be $110.00 for the workshop, if paid in advance.  Friday night at the door will $30.00 and Saturday at the door will be $95.00.  Attached is a registration form with more details: 2012 Workshop with Sifu Fong Ha.

There will be a recording of the workshop that will be available in September/October time-frame for a minimal cost.  This years workshop will be a great learning experience in a very nice and peaceful environment.  Looking forward to seeing you there.

item.title: Fall 2008 Workshop
item.description:
We will practice the Zhan Zhuang standing meditation. Learn movements from Taiji (Traditional Yang style) and Yiquan, by applying the three steps of cultivation, manifestation, and utilization we will see how one can turn simple movement into refined skill.

  • Ba Duan Jin – Eight Pieces of the Brocade
  • Yi Jin Jing – Muscle/Sinew Changing Exercises
  • Taiji Ruler – Bring your rulers
  • Effortless Push Hands

Sifu Fong Ha started his formal training in Tai Chi Chuan in 1953 first with Master Tung Ying-Chieh and after his death with Master Yang Sau-Chung, the eldest son of the legendary Yang Cheng-fu. His training in the Internal System of Martial Arts, Yiquan, was with Master Han Xing-Yuan, a disciple of Master Wang Hsin-Chai, the founder of Yiquan also known as Dachenquan. Sifu Ha is famous for his simple and clear way of teaching. He emphasizes the classic and the fundamental principles of Taiji. In this way the practitioner develops deep understanding of the art. He develops internal power that project outward into his everyday life.

Location and Times

  • Comfort Inn & Suites at 2437 S. Wildcat Way, Woods Cross, UT 84010
  • Sept. 26 -27, 2008
  • Friday, Sept. 26 at 7:00PM
  • Saturday, Sept. 27 at 9AM

Cost

  • $100 paid in advance
  • $130 on the day of the event ($30 for Friday/$100 for Saturday)
  • Make checks out to Don Stringham
